Consider slightly longer warm-up for cardio

AA.effect: Significantly reduced beta-2 response
AA.interpretation: Homozygous - substantially lower bronchodilator response and fat mobilization during exercise This is a statistical association that interacts with lifestyle, environment, and other genetic factors.

Consider inhaled beta-2 if prescribed
Longer cardio sessions may be more effective
Fat oxidation may be reduced during high intensity

C/C.interpretation: Your ADRB2 genotype is one of several genetic factors linked to r16g. Research in this area continues to evolve, and most traits have multiple genetic and environmental contributors.
recommendations: Stay informed as new research clarifies the role of ADRB2 in r16g
Consider discussing with a healthcare provider familiar with genetic interpretation

humans. Science. 1999 Jan 8;283(5399):212-4.

tissue thermogenesis in humans. International Journal of Obesity.
Oomen JM, van Rossum CTM, et al. β2-Adrenergic Receptor Polymorphisms and Salbutamol-Stimulated
Energy Expenditure. The Journal of Clinical Endocrinology & Metabolism, Volume 90, Issue 4, 1 April 2005,
Reguero M, Gómez de Cedrón M, et al. Precision Nutrition to Activate Thermogenesis as a Complementary
Approach to Target Obesity and Associated-Metabolic-Disorders. Cancers (Basel). 2021 Feb; 13(4): 866.
Kiani AK, Medori MC, et al. Clinical assessment for diet prescription. J Prev Med Hyg. 2022 Oct 17;63(2
Ricquier D. Fundamental mechanisms of thermogenesis. Comptes Rendus. Biologies, Volume 329 (2006)
rs1042714.technical_report:
Some of the latest scientific studies have shown an increasing interest in the ADRB2 and

ADRB3 genes because of their role in the regulation of thermogenesis and energy
metabolism, especially in the context of brown adipose tissue (BAT) and obesity. The β2-adrenergic receptor (β2-AR), encoded by the ADRB2 gene, is involved in the
thermogenic and lipolytic response. Variations in the ADRB2 gene have been associated
with BAT function and thermogenesis. These variations also affect glucose homeostasis
and may influence susceptibility to obesity and cardiovascular disease. In particular, ADRB2
genotypes could influence the function of blood vessels near the BAT, thus affecting
hemodynamics and body temperature. Specific ADRB2 variants, such as rs1042713, have shown correlations with thermogenic
response and blood flow regulation in the BAT. These variants affect the blood flow
response to isoproterenol, a beta agonist, which may influence the thermogenic capacity of
the BAT and thus the regulation of body temperature and energy balance. On the other hand, the β3-adrenergic receptor (β3-AR), encoded by the ADRB3 gene, is
crucial for BAT activation and non-shivering thermogenesis. This receptor is predominantly

 expressed in brown and white adipose tissue. Activation of β3-AR in brown adipocytes leads
to increased fatty acid oxidation and heat production, contributing significantly to energy
expenditure and body weight regulation. Although the relationship of these genes with temperature regulation has been known for
years, the scientific evidence is still somewhat limited and more studies are needed to
evaluate this association in depth, especially in different populations.
rs1042714.description:
PDF

Heat production in response to cold
Thermogenesis is a vital process for the maintenance of body temperature and energy
balance in mammals. Research on BAT (brown adipose tissue) and its activation pathways
offers promising prospects for the treatment and prevention of obesity and its related
metabolic complications.

rs1042713.name: Arg16Gly

GG.interpretation: For arg16gly, your ADRB2 variant is one contributing factor — beta-2 adrenergic receptor gly16 — greater receptor downregulation with prolonged agonist stimulation; relevant for bronchodilator response in asthma and sympathomimetic response during exercise
recommendations: If using beta-2 agonist inhalers (asthma): tolerance develops faster; discuss step-up therapy with physician
High-intensity interval training may be less stimulant-dependent for performance due to receptor sensitivity
Caffeine (indirect sympathomimetic) may have attenuated ergogenic effect compared to Arg16 individuals

AG.interpretation: For arg16gly, your ADRB2 variant is one contributing factor — intermediate adrb2 downregulation phenotype
recommendations: Standard ergogenic supplementation guidelines apply

AA.effect: Arg16/Arg16 — sustained beta-2 receptor response
AA.interpretation: For arg16gly, your ADRB2 variant is one contributing factor — slower receptor downregulation — more sustained beta-2 agonist response; better long-term bronchodilator response and potentially greater catecholamine effect during sustained exercise
recommendations: Caffeine ergogenic effect may be more pronounced (sustained receptor availability)
Consider regular versus as-needed inhaler strategy with physician if asthmatic (Arg16 shows better sustained response to LABA)

A/A.interpretation: Your ADRB2 genotype is one of several genetic factors linked to arg16gly. Research in this area continues to evolve, and most traits have multiple genetic and environmental contributors.
recommendations: Stay informed as new research clarifies the role of ADRB2 in arg16gly
Consider discussing with a healthcare provider familiar with genetic interpretation

thermogenic and lipolytic response. Variations in the ADRB2 gene have been associated
